originalPrompt:
Framework: Express, Port: 4566, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: Create a simple flash card app, using only red, black, and white colors. Make it minimal, and simple. add the function to add/delete/modify. frontend + backend needed

Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- port: 4566
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None

subtasks.title: Initialize Node project and repo
subtasks.description: Create project folder, run npm init -y, and add .gitignore. Install deps: express, cors, uuid. Install dev deps: nodemon, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, concurrently. Create src and public directories.
subtasks.successCriteria: package.json and node_modules exist with listed deps; src and public folders created; git repo initialized.

subtasks.title: Setup Express server on port 4566
subtasks.description: Create src/server.js to initialize Express with express.json(), CORS, and static serving of ./public. Add /health route and listen explicitly on port 4566.
subtasks.successCriteria: Server starts without errors; GET http://localhost:4566/health returns OK; static index.html is served.

subtasks.title: Implement in-memory flashcard CRUD API
subtasks.description: Add /api/cards routes: GET list, POST create, PUT update by :id, DELETE by :id. Use an in-memory array with uuid ids and fields {id, question, answer}. Return proper status codes and JSON.
subtasks.successCriteria: All CRUD endpoints respond with expected payloads and codes; basic error responses for missing fields or not found.

subtasks.title: Configure Tailwind CSS with restricted colors
subtasks.description: Initialize Tailwind via npx tailwindcss init -p. Set content to scan public/**/*.html and public/**/*.js. Configure theme colors to only red, black, and white. Create src/styles.css with Tailwind directives and build to public/styles.css.
subtasks.successCriteria: public/styles.css is generated; Tailwind classes apply; only red, black, and white are used in styles.

subtasks.title: Build minimal HTML UI for flashcards
subtasks.description: Create public/index.html with a clean layout using Tailwind. Include a form to add or edit a card (question and answer) and a list rendering area with edit and delete controls. Use only red, black, and white colors.
subtasks.successCriteria: Page loads with minimal UI, required inputs, and buttons; visual style uses only the specified colors.

subtasks.title: Implement client-side JS to call API
subtasks.description: Create public/main.js to fetch and render cards, handle form submit to POST, support edit flow with PUT, and delete with DELETE. Update the UI on success and show basic loading states.
subtasks.successCriteria: Users can add, modify, and delete cards from the UI with immediate updates and persisted state in memory.

subtasks.title: Add validation and error handling
subtasks.description: Add server-side validation for non-empty question and answer with length limits and consistent error JSON. Add client-side checks and show inline error messages in red, keeping design minimal.
subtasks.successCriteria: Invalid inputs are blocked with clear messages; API responds with 400 for bad requests; no uncaught errors in console or server.

subtasks.title: Perform manual and API smoke testing
subtasks.description: Manually test add, edit, delete, and refresh flows. Verify only allowed colors appear. Add a simple Node script or curl commands to smoke test CRUD endpoints and confirm response shapes.
subtasks.successCriteria: All CRUD flows work reliably; API smoke tests pass; UI remains minimal and color-limited.

subtasks.title: Add npm scripts for dev, build, and start
subtasks.description: Add scripts: tailwind:build, tailwind:watch, dev (concurrently run nodemon and tailwind watch), build (tailwind build), and start (node server). Document PORT 4566 in scripts and README.
subtasks.successCriteria: npm run dev serves on http://localhost:4566 with live CSS; npm run build generates CSS; npm start runs server successfully.

decompositionStrategy: Backend-first API with parallel Tailwind setup, then minimal UI implementation and integration, followed by validation and testing.
